purpose: Two monotonic gains the NEXT parent inherits without re-earning them: (A) BPW potency, (B) decision speed. Everything here is a measured campaign artifact or an explicitly labelled prediction. Nothing here is a capability claim.

richest_legal_arm_for_this_inventory.note: the most rate the 1.0 ceiling buys at keep-128; everything richer (S128_g1.25_d0.625 at 1.051903792) is ILLEGAL and was never run

lowest_FUNCTIONAL_bpw.verdict: NO FUNCTIONAL RATE EXISTS ON THIS PARENT AT OR BELOW 1.0 COMPLETE BPW
evidence: S1 full forward, 94 layers, 6 frozen holdout prompts, cached parent logits: S64_structural 0.948410027 BPW symKL 8.599 argmax 0.0735 cos 0.434 top5 0.080
S64_doctor 0.999769787 BPW symKL 7.699 argmax 0.0922 cos 0.468 top5 0.143
gate wants symKL <= 0.10 and argmax >= 0.95: COLLAPSE 12/12
earlier fixed-weight PQ/VQ family: argmax EXACTLY 0.0 at 1.0075 and at 0.4930 BPW
lowest_FUNCTIONAL_bpw.inheritance: the next parent must NOT assume a functional sub-1.0 rate exists; it must PROVE one at F1 before any budget is spent past F1

best_organ_allocation.rule: buy gate_proj and up_proj at 4x the index rate of down_proj
best_organ_allocation.why: at equal complete bits the gamma-anisotropic organs (gate_proj, up_proj - both read the post-attention-LayerNorm output) convert bits into output-error reduction faster than down_proj, whose input is the SwiGLU intermediate

per_cell_codec_mode.free_structural_rule: gvq for gate_proj and up_proj, vq for down_proj
per_cell_codec_mode.measured_exceptions: 2 of 27 measured cells move off the free rule, BOTH at layer 0 (gate_proj and up_proj -> kronecker, concentration 0.999497, up_proj output 0.014315 vs gvq 0.081316)
per_cell_codec_mode.inheritance: do NOT ship a 2-bit per-cell mode field. Ship the free structural rule plus a hardcoded layer-0 kronecker exception, and re-test that exception on the new parent's layer 0 only.

adaptive_k_distribution.method: exact byte auction over the 1.0 complete-BPW ceiling (qwen_adaptive_k)
adaptive_k_distribution.status: PREDICTED under the packer's own predictor, NOT forward-measured
adaptive_k_distribution.inheritance: the auction MECHANISM transfers (spend the ceiling where the predictor says marginal error per bit is steepest); the K numbers themselves are Qwen-topology-bound and must be re-auctioned per parent

routing_coverage.withdrawn_figure: the 92.2 pct keep-64 retention from the 88-token calibration is WITHDRAWN - it was collected ON the scored prompts
routing_coverage.calibration_trust: 1313 tokens is NOT trustworthy for per-expert allocation: only 81.0 pct of hot/cold assignments survive resampling at 95 pct and 2.1 pct of experts are never routed; a stable median partition needs ~7448 tokens
routing_coverage.inheritance: budget >= 7448 calibration tokens BEFORE any per-expert allocation, or use routing only as a coarse quartile aggregate

doctor_recovery_per_bit.mechanism_CORRECTED: the ROW SELECTOR is not the mechanism - random rows recover 96-99 pct of the gain; the SECOND CODEBOOK STAGE earns the bytes
doctor_recovery_per_bit.inheritance: transfer the second-stage capacity, NOT the selector heuristic; and note that 10.5 pct off 8.599 is still 77x the 0.10 gate - Doctor at this potency cannot close a collapse, only decorate one

dead_representations.evidence: best single survivor median held-out rel err 0.885/0.993/0.995 at L0/L3/L7; 4-survivor LS 0.863/0.988/0.995; ~1.0 is the trivial zero predictor
masked 0.0784 beats bias 0.0993, low-rank r8 0.1176, full survivor-row retune 0.0927; the KL term is provably INERT (softmax restricted to survivors IS the renormalized teacher)
dead_representations.reopen: large survivor-restricted oracle gap AND a student that generalizes to held-out ids

dead_representations.evidence: row-normalized mean off-diagonal expert cosine 0.00168 (raw 0.00166) - orthogonal in both spaces; cross-layer same-index tying indistinguishable from a different-index control at 1e-7
quantization error is HIGH rank; top-64 SVD captures 3-9 pct of energy; rank-64 healed TQ3 0.114 -> 0.104 for 4.6 bpw
dead_representations.reopen: residual MEASURED low rank on the parent in question

dead_representations.evidence: Lloyd index distribution is close to uniform by construction; the coder returns less than its own table costs
argmax EXACTLY 0.0 at both 1.0075 and 0.4930 BPW; 7 ladder rungs RETIRED (not deleted)

under a genuinely disjoint probe (capture 0.058-0.099) mean gain -1.92 pct over 13 low-capture expert-cells, 1 of 13 positive; the sealed +11.4 pct reproduces only on the ORIGINAL position-level split (capture 1.000/0.991/0.422). Root cause: the corpus repeats 12 segments so 1313 positions carry 540 UNIQUE ids and layer-0 MoE input is a pure function of the id - the same embedding row was in both halves
dead_representations.reopen: a corpus with enough unique ids to give a large fit span AND capture <= 0.1

D1_route_only.setup: perfect bf16 experts, router masked to the same 64

D2_recon_only.setup: full 128 router, experts packed at survivor rate

structural_finding_that_outranks_all_rat.inheritance: TEST REPRESENTATION QUALITY BEFORE SPENDING ANYTHING ON ROUTING POLICY

measured_costs_this_campaign.parent_logits: cached once, reused across EVERY pass - the single largest saving in the campaign; a parent forward is never re-run to score a candidate
measured_costs_this_campaign.loop_order: expert-OUTER / candidate-INNER: each expert tensor is read from disk once and every candidate scores against it before it is freed
measured_costs_this_campaign.spec_memoization_mechanism: identical (family, dim, k, stages) specs recur across candidates; memoizing the packed result across the candidate-inner loop removed 33 pct of pack work

dead_method_time_avoided.label: ESTIMATE from per-rung pack cost, not a stopwatch

derived_planning_numbers.label: DERIVED ESTIMATES from the measured per-layer costs above, not separately timed
derived_planning_numbers.implication: full fidelity is ~1.5 h per candidate. A 6-candidate tournament is affordable ONLY because F0-F4 kill most candidates before they reach it.

process_levers_that_transfer: cache parent logits once per (prompt set, parent) and never recompute
expert-outer / candidate-inner tensor loop; <= 4 expert tensors resident, freed eagerly
memoize packed results by exact spec across candidates (33 pct measured)
retire a rung UNRUN when its mechanism is already sealed dead - 7 rungs, ~7 h
exact Fraction bit accounting at PROPOSAL time, so no illegal candidate is ever packed
adversarial re-score of every positive result BEFORE it is inherited - 4 of this campaign's headline claims were corrected or withdrawn by that pass
process_failures_that_cost_time: fit/score splits asserted rather than proven disjoint: S3A and S2C both produced headline gains that a disjointness audit erased. Cost: two full sub-campaigns.

a green self-check over a dead code path: S3D's self-check asserted fixed_rule_mode('up_proj') == 'gvq' and passed while RUNGS had no 'up_proj' key, so measure_cell raised KeyError and 0 of 18 cells were up_proj - 6016 of 18048 coded expert tensors went unmeasured. ALWAYS make the self-check call the shipping path.
weight-space proxies scored for months before the first real forward; the forward then collapsed 12/12. Buy the cheapest real forward FIRST.

honesty: Weight-space and activation-proxy error are NOT capability. The only capability-fidelity measurements in this campaign are the S1 full forward and the D1/D2 controls, and all of them are collapses.
Every gain in section A is either (i) forward-measured and still short of the gate, (ii) predicted under a packer's own predictor, or (iii) a proxy on a calibration batch. Each is labelled inline.
The section B derived numbers are arithmetic on measured per-layer costs, not stopwatch measurements of whole runs.
